user_query:
I run a small mutual-aid discussion space, and I really don’t want to silence survivors sharing resources while still catching posts that describe or promote violence. I have a simple keyword-based classifier in this workspace that I use to flag violent content. Please help me by adding a small, reliable test suite and tuning the configuration so the tests pass in a way that reflects these priorities.

What I need:
1) Create tests/test_filter.py that:
   - Imports the classifier from src/filter.py and loads the configuration from config/terms.json.
   - Reads all labeled examples from data/sample_reports.jsonl and, for each record, asserts that classify_text(text, cfg) matches the provided boolean label field.
   - Includes at least one additional assertion demonstrating that decreasing the threshold (in an in-memory copy of the loaded config) would cause at least one survivor-support example to be mis-flagged, to show why the chosen threshold matters. Do not modify files on disk in this additional assertion; just work with a copy.

2) Update config/terms.json so that all tests in (1) pass. Specifically:
   - Raise the threshold to 2.
   - Add an exceptions list that reduces false positives for survivor-support posts: ["support group", "hotline", "shelter", "resources"].
   - Do not change the violent_terms list provided in the file.

3) Edit docs/README.md by appending a new section titled "Violence filter tests" that summarizes:
   - The threshold value you ended up with (expected: 2).
   - The exceptions you added (the four phrases above).
   - A short note that classification is based on the presence of any of the violent terms, reduced by the presence of any listed exceptions, and flagged when the adjusted count >= threshold.
   - A compact summary derived from data/sample_reports.jsonl: total number of cases (8), how many are expected to be flagged true (4), and how many false (4).

Deliverables:
- tests/test_filter.py (new)
- config/terms.json (updated in place)
- docs/README.md (updated with the new section)

Please rely only on the files in this workspace. The tests should pass deterministically after the configuration change, and the README summary should reflect the dataset and configuration above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
